** script.sh was generated by AI to accomplish the following, use at your own risk **

wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/angristan/openvpn-install/master/openvpn-install.sh
chmod +x openvpn-install.sh
sudo ./openvpn-install.sh install

sudo apt install libpam-google-authenticator
sudo nano /etc/pam.d/openvpn
```bash
# Standard password check (optional, if you want password + 2FA)
# @include common-auth
# @include common-account

# The Google Authenticator requirement
auth required pam_google_authenticator.so
```

sudo nano /etc/openvpn/server/server.conf
```bash
# Enable PAM for 2FA
plugin /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/openvpn/plugins/openvpn-plugin-auth-pam.so openvpn

# Force the client to show a 2FA prompt
static-challenge "Enter Google Auth Code: " 1
```

sudo nano /etc/openvpn/server/server.conf
```bash
port 443
proto tcp
```

nano client.opvn
```
proto tcp
remote x.x.x.x 443
auth-user-pass
```

sudo systemctl restart openvpn-server@server

google-authenticator

sudo mkdir -p /etc/openvpn/2fa
sudo mv /home/user/.google_authenticator /etc/openvpn/2fa/user
sudo chown nobody:nogroup /etc/openvpn/2fa/user
sudo chmod 400 /etc/openvpn/2fa/user

sudo nano /etc/pam.d/openvpn
```bash
auth required pam_google_authenticator.so secret=/etc/openvpn/2fa/${USER} user=root
```

# Use openvpn client (Recommended)
